Summary: Possible TSB Issues | FTWR
Description:
We gathered logs in FTWR on a STB with a debug build. 

These are the findings from the log analysis:

Based on log analysis, RF layer is pristine ({*}zero FEC errors, stable SNR, locked tuner{*}) and the *video decoder reports zero* errors across 57 minutes of continuous monitoring. But the transport stream is severely corrupted: *68,930 TS discontinuity errors* accumulated on PID 7281 during the capture (~20/sec), and *5,414 Bad CRC32 sections* on PID 0x1C71 (~1.6/sec).  This corruption is on the DSGCC (DSG Client Controller) proxy path, not the video PID,  which explains why the video decoder is *unaffected* during this particular capture. The SDV carousel is in a failure state (falling back to cache), and the DSGCC proxy is processing massively corrupted transport data on PID 7281/0x1C71. While these PIDs are not the video stream, this level of transport corruption indicates the FTWR-2 delivery infrastructure has systemic data integrity issues beyond just the video path.

 

Orion provided further detail: 

The PID 7281 (0x1c71) is most likely related to the data carousel coming from TSB, so check that first before you go to controller. Video Decoder did not have any errors in the latest log, so we will be waiting for reproduction of the error with its reflection in the log.
Custom field (Last Comment):
The two PIDs were enabled on the TSB, but the change was rolled back. 

I will enter another Hazcon today for those to be re-enabled so that we can gather STB logs after the change is implemented.

Description:
Creating this ticket to investigate following issue reported to VPO via email:
Acc # 8448200010355591
 
| | |


Good Morning and Happy Holidays Mr. Winfrey,


As long time Time Warner/Spectrum customers that bundle our cable, TV and landline services, we are very upset with the service to fix our DVR box.  After 3 attempts by Spectrum repairmen coming to fix the problem by replacing 3 DVR boxes, we still have no working DVR!
We were gone for several days only to come home and find all the shows we'd recorded were erased and, showing we used 102% of our recording space!  We had this issue last month about the % of recorded programs and called in and were told it was fixed, only to find out it wasn't...this started then....
 
After receiving 3 automated calls from Spectrum informing us the problem was fixed, seeing trucks and many repairmen several times on our street _*and*_ being told they fixed an underground cable issue, we still don't have a working DVR box, which we pay for!!
Please contact us and let us know when/how this problem will be fixed.  We wanted to play several recorded programs for our family during the holidays, but of course were unable to.
We thank you in advance and hope you were take care of this immediately.
Sincerely,
Barry L. and Roberta H. Berson

Summary: [MSE] Xumo:  large button remote does NOT control "LG C1 series(OLED77C1PUB)" TV volume. Power button does function. Xumo PR1 remote has no issue controlling my TV volume or power.
Description:
*Issue:* Xumo:  large button remote does NOT control "LG C1 series(OLED77C1PUB)" TV volume. Power button does function. Xumo PR1 remote has no issue controlling my TV volume or power.

Account: 8260140212799607

*Issue description:*
* I brought home a new, unboxed Xumo Large Button remote. Set up was very easy as it only asked me to press the voice button on the remote and it paired automatically. It then asked if I would like to control my TV to which I said yes. After its search it located 1 TV option and asked me to check if it worked properly by adjusting the volume, which did not work. It then moved on to the next option (option 0 it advised?) which also did not work. After selecting No it advised my TV was not supported. I tried the process again, this time advising I would manually select my TV, but the model, or any similar model, was not an option (TV model LG77C1PUB).
* I went through the process again, this time selecting option 1 DID work despite it failing to control the volume during testing. The remote will successfully control the TV's power but will not adjust volume.
* Also note, my original PR1 Xumo remote that came with the Xumo will control my TV's volume and power without issue.

*Approximate date and time when the issue was last observed.*
This was last observed on 3/23 (I believe), when I initially attempted the set up and submitted the MSE feedback.

*Device MAC address if the issue is observed on Xumo / Traditional STB.*
Xumo MAC: 58:76:AC:D6:D3:B7

Summary: SCI000012575750 - stba-8104 on 3 STB's. Failing to stage or unreachable
Custom field (Resolution / Mitigation So:
Summary:
New install (3/9) on account 8413120620019489 with 4 STBs — 1 staged successfully (3C9A77722C3C), 3 failed to stage (DCA6335F241C, B0B3AD9E4CE8, 0C7FB23309AC). All 3 unstaged boxes presenting "Spectrum TV Unavailable" / error 8104/8124. Root cause identified as CAS/DAC enrollment and EMM delivery failures. BOI-15956 created and closed.

Troubleshooting:

Staging hits sent (hit/ref/coll/boot) — EMMs not updating on 3 boxes post-cycle

Power cycle, connection check, and cache clear performed — no change

CAS diagnostics (cas diag / silentdiag) run on all unstaged MACs:

B0B3AD9E4CE8: CA Status Not Ready, 0 EMMs received, Hub ID = 0 at CAS layer (profile shows 14 — mismatch), billingid/acntnum/hub_id blank in NVM — controller unable to address box, likely not enrolled in DAC or wrong unit address

DCA6335F241C: CA Status Not Ready, 85 EMMs received / 35 valid, Last Error 0x1F1D (CAS EMM processing failure) — EMMs arriving but failing validation, CA record mismatch suspected (wrong chip ID or unit address in DAC); box offline at time of DOJ follow-up

0C7FB23309AC: Unreachable — same class of issue suspected

BOI-15956 created to verify DNCS enrollment and send billing hits — closed confirming not a billing issue; EC team noted EMMs must be loaded via Supply Chain/staging process

Escalated to DOJ team for on-site confirmation; DCA6335F241C confirmed offline via SpecNav at time of DOJ handoff

Resolution:
Pending customer verification. DOJ team engaged to confirm with customer whether issue is resolved and identify any MACs still affected. Supply Chain contact required for all 3 unstaged boxes to reload EMMs through proper staging process. Ticket pending DOJ/customer confirmation response.

Summary: SCI000012571752 - Long Delays while tuning on Samsung TV STVA
Custom field (Resolution / Mitigation So:
Summary:
Customer experiencing 18-20 second channel change delay on Samsung TV (model: 23_PONTUSM_QD_OS80, OS: 2024.0) using Spectrum TV app. Roku app on same TV performed normally. DPSTRIAGE-88207 / POSTRCA-30471 referenced for prior identical issue on 2019 Samsung (closed as expected behavior).

Troubleshooting:

Tech visit completed prior to PAC contact — issue persisted post-visit

Confirmed issue isolated to Samsung TV; Roku app on same network performed normally, ruling out connectivity/account issue

STB software: 2022.0 | Customer on wireless connection via account-registered modem | Third-party router in use

Masquerade used to replicate issue

Log analysis identified: uriObtainedMs: 2,362ms (elevated), SERIALIZE_FIRST_TUNE config active, videoCodec: hevc, drmType: playReady

Cross-referenced POSTRCA-30471 (2019 Samsung, identical symptom — closed as expected behavior); Dev team engaged to determine if current case warrants new POSTRCA

Follow-up log review showed tune times fully normalized — assessed as likely impacted by a backend delivery change

Resolution:
Contacted customer and confirmed issue is fully resolved. Ticket closed with customer confirmation. Backend delivery change identified as probable cause; no further action required. Dev team engagement ongoing regarding POSTRCA determination.

Summary: CLONE - SCI000012577294 - SG : "GDVR-1001" error throws when trying to cancel future recordings
Custom field (Resolution / Mitigation So:
1. obtained the full list of Cassandra tables from IPVC and used Amazon Q to  identify which table stores completed recordings. Q analyzed the table  definitions and identified the most likely  table.
2.Requested IPVC to pull sample data from that table to cross-verify.
3.IPVC's data confirmed the table Q identified was correct.
4.Used Q to generate the delete query for that table and provided it to IPVC  for execution.
5.Verified via emulator — 150+ old recordings (dating back to 2020, for the new Connect Box) were successfully cleared. 🙂

Summary: SPECU: Blue screen every night between 3:00 AM and 4:00 AM
Description:
!Device_ID.jpg!

 

The Spectrum Enterprise TV app is the only app we have on our ROKUs. All data savers, timers, screensavers, etc. have been turned off and still, every night between 3:00am and 4:00am all four of our TVs go to that error code blue screen for almost exactly an hour, and then they just automatically go to the ROKU Home Page. I have done all of the things that ROKU suggests online such as powering down the unit for two minutes, doing a system restart and even a factory reset. Nothing helps, that is why I believe it must be something with the Spectrum Enterprise app.
Custom field (Last Comment): I spoke with Roku developer who confirmed their next scheduled app release is targeted for March 31st. They plan to triage this issue and attempt to include a fix in that release for app not auto retrying.

Care team,

which is an expected behavior when risky behavior was detected, and the transaction was blocked.
Next step:
Please ask the customer to reset new/fresh password with username (larry613@spectrum.net) and also
connect to spectrum modem/router and make sure they have disabled any firewall or adblocker or any other firewall /security software and have the customer retry login in fresh session after clearing cache and cookies.

Additional Note:
Here are a few suggestions to prevent IDID-3210, if that is still occurring,
1. Agents to not attempt login with the customers’ credentials (If you attempt to log in for the customer it may cause the issue to get worse)
2. Agents to ask customers to wait 24 hours before attempting login again
3. Customer to attempt login while behind the modem/from home if they have Spectrum internet
4. Customer to attempt login on a device they were able to successfully login with before
5. Customer to attempt login while connected to a network that they were able to successfully login while connected to before

Steps To activate Streaming app through Spectrum.

1. Sign In: Go to Spectrum.net on a web browser or open the My Spectrum App and sign in with your Primary Spectrum username and password.
2. Find Your Services: Navigate to the TV/Streaming section of your account; you might see a "Services" or "View Current Plan" tab.
3. Locate Streaming Apps: Scroll down to find "Your Streaming Apps" or a similar section showing eligible services like Disney+, Paramount, ESPN+, or Max (formerly HBO Max).
4. Start Activation: Click the "Activate" button or arrow next to the specific app you want to use.
5. Create/Link Account: You'll be prompted to enter the email and password you want to use for that streaming service, or sign in with your existing credentials if you already have an account, and follow the on-screen instructions.
6. Download & Log In: Download the actual app (e.g., paramount, Max) on your device (phone, smart TV, etc.) and log in using the email and password you just set up during the activation process.

2.Checked issue with Onshore and got update as:
This looks like a DVR trick play buffering issue on Roku, not a general streaming or ad-related problem. The user was heavily fast-forwarding/rewinding through a recording from their library, and the player was struggling to resume playback after each trick play action.

Resolution: Educated the cx as Roku device is almost 10 years old as it was released in the 2017.Also, it was hammering on trick play as it was overloading for the device as it is old device.
Propably needed an upgrade

Summary: Incorrect DSP on Cable Cards Post Network Evolution Upgrade
Custom field (Resolution / Mitigation So: Updated DSP plant in CSG
Description:
Research INC000035925594.

It is a Q2A CableCARD DSP issue. We are looking to see if there is a configuration issue at the DAC level or further upstream.

Can you please take look and let us know who owns this, what the process should be, and how do we resolve issues?

 

Issue: We had an Anytime Fitness that had lost channel after Network Evolution upgrade (Juneau hub) yesterday. This customer is a Q2A that lost all their channels. I submitted a DOJ intake ticket when I was brough aware, assuming they needed a remap, like most do..

 

Account: 8285123500268361

SPRING ST, BEAVER DAM, WI 53916//8285123500268361 ECT-86140862

 

This is from NFS on the chat that was created after the intake for was submitted:  !image-2026-02-06-10-20-15-849.png!  

 

They flipped the units to DSG and flipped the cable cards and sent hits - both units did not have a map associated to download, and they both looked like this:

 

NFS then said " could be the downstream plant the cable cards are pointing to. *We have had two area in the last two days with incorrect DSP on the cards after cuts.* and it has to be manually updated in the DAC and we do not have access to update it

 

This is what it was:

!image-2026-02-06-10-20-15-927.png!

And this is what it should have been:

!image-2026-02-06-10-20-15-998.png!

INC ticket was opened to have DSP changed and then NFS was able to map channels to MPEG4.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Notes from INC

2/4/2026 2:37:55 PM

"We are requesting this change be completed asap as customer has been without video for 2 weeks."

2/4/2026 4:38:03 PM

"Teams continue to delete and readd cable cards within the DAC"

2/4/2026 5:37:12 PM

"Preventable Cause: nodes were transitioned to DAA and there was no confirmation post work that CableCard video services were working
Resolution: CableCards were removed and re-added to the DAC, cards were re-initialized and channel lineups were re-added. Field Operations has confirmed that channels have been restored."
Custom field (Last Comment):
Closing ticket. 

I worked with VO and found Enterprise contacts that could fill in the details.

 

Very short summary: The account was not high split ready as expected prior to this maintenance due to equipment not being swapped for high-split ready equipment. Enterprise team worked to mitigate the impacted customer in this case and 33 others. Enterprise and other stake holders are investigating how these types of issues can be caught sooner.

 

Paul Reilly’s full reply below:
The subject of notifying customers about MPEG-4 & DAA-RPHY cuts has been discussed across Client Services and Tech Support dating back to beginnings of the High Split project in 2023 when it expanded to multiple markets (Reno was the 1{^}st{^}). Each time it has come up, leadership has concluded that maintenance windows are BAU and due to some businesses having multiple properties, pinpointing exact service disruptions can be challenging.

 

Specific to the Anytime Fitness account in Beaver Dam, WI – The CSG billing account # 8285123500032544 is segmented as SMB and was to be managed by the SMB Product team when we were 2 distinct teams in 2023. In the Fall of 2025, my team began reviewing accounts that had not been mitigated, Anytime Fitness and 33 other “SMB” accounts were not High Split ready as initially agreed to 3 years ago.

 

An account is considered mitigated when it has TC600Es installed to maintain the same channel lineup in a High Split environment as they have had for years prior. The location had 1 Vecima TC600 delivering 30 channels. With DAA-RPHY (and previous hub cuts) node changes, channels are delivered in HD & MPEG-4. Due to the limitations of the media processors TC600s cannot process SDV, HD nor MPEG-4 content.

 

To maintain 30 channels – 2 TC600Es are required.

 

Our team, together with Business Planning, Supply Chain & Field Ops arranged for the installation of 2 new TC600Es on a new Account # 8285123500268361. This allowed for the TC600 to work in parallel while the new units were being configured. It also was the 1{^}st{^} step in migrating all remaining active MDTA accounts to Mid-Large Business as Small Business has a history of not being able to support these devices and typically transferring TCs to MLTS.

 

New TC600Es were installed on Acct # 8285123500268361 in November 2025.  This new account is classified as “ENT Equipment” in CSG.

 

I received an email dated 2/3/26 on 2/4/26 about the Anytime Fitness being hard down. From there, a chat was set up with NFS/DOJ teams to remote access the units to determine root cause of channel loss.

 

The main issues as outlined by Becky Kohlman (Regional FO’s PM):

 

1 – The DSP was incorrect after the node was upgraded (We discussed this today on Nikki Jo Net Evo call, and I am asking how this can be avoided in the future – this was sent to Kathy Hoffman-Gilles today – attached the email I sent).

2 – These need a remap after the node cut.  [@Reilly, Paul V|mailto:Paul.Reilly@charter.com] , if the node cuts are in archer, how do we get visibility to enter a intake for to get them remapped?  Or are you doing that on the side.  [@Haraqui, Ermira|mailto:C-Ermira.Haraqui@charter.com] how about on your side?

 

 

My reply is in the attached which outlines an ongoing issue with reporting when node dates are scheduled to take place. In 2025, regular weekly meetings were held with Program Management team to review impacts to accounts with Cable Cards (MDTAs, Q2Qs or “Stand-Alone” cards mainly in residential accounts with TiVo or similar products). Those meetings have been sporadic since the beginning of the year due to reporting gaps. That issue is now being worked out with Business Integration and Business Intelligence, which to Becky’s point above would have been caught and addressed via an Intake ticket to reconfigure & re-map the units. The transition should have been seamless or with minimum downtime.

 

In my discussions with NFS/DOJ – the issues are more prevalent in DAC Controllers vs. EC but Becky did share with Kathy Hoffman-Gillis so that all teams can take proactive steps for node cuts.

 

Please let me know of any additional questions – this was an isolated case & stakeholders now are on the lookout for these types of accounts.
